title: Multiply 2-digit numbers with area models
description: Use an area model to decompose factors and multiply.
topic_path: Get ready for multiplication and division with whole numbers and decimals
Multi-digit multiplication: place value and area models

assessment_items.question_content:
The following area model shows $24 \times 32$ broken into parts.   
*The image is not drawn to scale.*

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part.**

 | |
-: | - | :-
$A$  | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$B$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$C$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
$D$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]

**What is the total area?**  
*Be sure to answer all parts of the question.*

$24 \times 32=$ [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints:
The area of the whole rectangle is $24 \times 32$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the area of the whole, we add the area of each part.

$\goldE{A}+\purpleD{B}+\maroonD{C} + \blueE{D}=24\times 32$

Now we add the areas to find the total.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{600}+\purpleD{40} +\maroonD{120}+\blueE{8}=768$

So $24 \times 32 =768$.

These are the areas:

$\begin{array}{rcr}
A&=&600\\\\
B&=&40\\\\
C&=&120\\\\
D&=&8\\\\
24\times 32 &= &768
\end{array}$

*Did you include all five parts of the answer?*

options.alt: An area model using a rectangle of 4 unequal parts. The first part, labeled A, has a height of 20 and a base of 30. A second part to the right, labeled B, has the same height as part A and a base of 2. A third part, labeled C, is directly below part A and has the same base as part A and a height of 4. A fourth part to the right, labeled D, has the same height as part C and a base of 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following area model shows $26 \times 74$ broken into parts.   
*The image is not drawn to scale.*

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part.**

 | |
-: | - | :-
$A$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$B$  | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$C$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
$D$ | $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]

**What is the total area?**  
*Be sure to answer all parts of the question.*

$26 \times 74=$ [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
hints:
The area of the whole rectangle is $26 \times 74$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the area of the whole, we add the area of each part.

$\goldE{A}+\purpleD{B}+\maroonD{C} + \blueE{D}=26\times 74$

Now we add the areas to find the total.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{1{,}400}+\purpleD{80} +\maroonD{420}+\blueE{24}=1{,}924$

So $26 \times 74 =1{,}924$.

B&=&80\\\\
C&=&420\\\\

options.alt: An area model using a rectangle of 4 unequal parts. The first part, labeled A, has a height of 20 and a base of 70. A second part to the right, labeled B, has the same height as part A and a base of 4. A third part, labeled C, is directly below part A and has the same base as part A and a height of 6. A fourth part to the right, labeled D, has the same height as part C and a base of 4.
